sections.paragraph:
Μανασσῆς, ῆ, acc. ῆ (-ῆν Mt 1:10 v.l.; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 12 Jac.; מְנַשֶּׁה) Manasseh (predominantly a Jewish name. But a Cyprian ins [OHoffmann, D. griech. Dialekte I 1891 p. 75 no. 140] gives it as the name of a Gr-Rom. as well. See ἀββᾶ, end.)

① firstborn son of Joseph (Gen 41:51; JosAs 21:8; Philo, Joseph.), father of a tribe 13:5 (cp. Gen 48:14). Of the tribe Rv 7:6.

② son of Hezekiah, Hebrew king (4 Km 21:1ff; 2 Ch 33:1ff; AscIs; Joseph.); in the genealogy of Jesus Mt 1:10; Lk 3:23ff D.—BHHW II 1136f (lit.).

acc. acc. = accusative

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Cyprian Cyprian , III a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 615
